codex_goal_prompt: Implement or repair a complete simulation-first tennis recognition and robot interception system. Start by auditing the repository and environment; then make a minimal runnable closed loop: synthetic tennis ball video or simulator -> detector -> trajectory and landing-point estimator -> robot planner/controller -> IMU-aided localization -> visualization/logging -> tests and README. Keep changes incremental and safe; prefer existing project structure; create missing modules only when needed; leave clear TODOs only for real hardware integration.
acceptance_criteria: A single smoke-test command runs the full simulated closed loop without hardware; it prints predicted landing point and robot final pose; generated plots or overlay video are saved under outputs/; tests pass or documented known failures are isolated.
validation_command: python -m tennis_robot_sim.run_sim --scenario default --no-hardware --output outputs/smoke || python scripts/run_sim.py --scenario default --no-hardware
files_to_inspect_or_create: entire repository; README*; environment.yml; requirements*.txt; pyproject.toml; setup.py; src/; sim/; scripts/; tests/
safety_constraints: Simulation-first only; do not trigger real motor/GPIO/serial writes unless an explicit --hardware or --real flag is passed; preserve local git changes; do not run destructive git reset/clean/rm commands.

goal_title: Synchronize repo safely and inventory project
codex_goal_prompt: Inspect the git repository state and project layout. Run git status, branch, remote, and log summary. If the repo is clean, run git pull --ff-only. If there are local changes, do not overwrite them; record them in environment_report.md and continue with read-only inventory. Identify README, entry points, dependency files, source packages, hardware-control files, and missing modules relevant to tennis detection, trajectory prediction, robot control, and IMU.
acceptance_criteria: environment_report.md contains git status, branch, remotes, latest commit, pull result or reason skipped, tree summary, detected Python entry points, and risk notes for hardware code.
validation_command: git status --short && git branch --show-current && git remote -v && find . -maxdepth 3 -type f | sed 's#^./##' | sort | head -300
files_to_inspect_or_create: .git/; README*; environment.yml; requirements*.txt; pyproject.toml; setup.py; src/; scripts/
safety_constraints: Never use git reset, git clean, or overwrite uncommitted files. If pull conflicts occur, stop and document them.

codex_goal_prompt: Make the Python environment reproducible. Inspect environment.yml, requirements.txt, pyproject.toml, setup.py, and import errors. Create scripts/setup_env.sh that creates or updates a conda env named tennis-robot-sim when possible. Prefer Python 3.10 or the project-specified version. Install editable package if pyproject/setup exists. Add a small scripts/check_env.py that imports cv2, numpy, scipy, matplotlib, yaml, torch if present, and project modules without crashing. Record CPU/GPU/Jetson/CUDA availability but do not require GPU for simulation.
acceptance_criteria: scripts/setup_env.sh and scripts/check_env.py exist; check_env.py prints OK for core simulation dependencies; environment_report.md lists exact Python, conda, pip, CUDA, and package versions.
files_to_inspect_or_create: environment.yml; requirements.txt; requirements-dev.txt; pyproject.toml; setup.py; scripts/
safety_constraints: Do not uninstall user packages globally. Do not require sudo. Keep CPU-only fallback working.

codex_goal_prompt: Ensure the project can be imported consistently. Detect whether code lives under src/, root packages, or scripts. Add minimal __init__.py files only where appropriate. Create a package namespace tennis_robot_sim if no clear package exists, otherwise adapt to the existing package. Add a lightweight module tennis_robot_sim.health with a health_check() function returning dependency and platform info.
acceptance_criteria: python -c 'import tennis_robot_sim; from tennis_robot_sim.health import health_check; print(health_check())' succeeds, or equivalent existing package import is documented in environment_report.md.
validation_command: python -c "import tennis_robot_sim; from tennis_robot_sim.health import health_check; print(health_check())"
safety_constraints: Minimize renames. Preserve existing APIs. Avoid moving hardware files unless tests prove imports require it.

codex_goal_prompt: Create a central config system for simulation parameters. Add configs/default_sim.yaml with camera intrinsics/extrinsics, tennis ball color thresholds, ball radius, gravity, bounce coefficient, court dimensions, robot kinematics, controller gains, IMU noise parameters, logging paths, and safety limits. Add tennis_robot_sim.config.load_config(path=None) that validates required keys and supplies defaults.
acceptance_criteria: load_config() returns a dict/dataclass with required sections: camera, detector, ball, court, robot, controller, imu, sim, logging, safety; missing optional keys receive defaults; invalid configs raise clear errors.
validation_command: python -c "from tennis_robot_sim.config import load_config; cfg=load_config(); print(cfg.keys() if hasattr(cfg,'keys') else cfg)"
safety_constraints: Simulation-first only; do not trigger real motor/GPIO/serial writes unless an explicit --hardware or --real flag is passed; preserve local git changes; do not run destructive git reset/clean/rm commands.

goal_title: Implement synthetic tennis ball and court simulator
codex_goal_prompt: Implement a deterministic simulation of tennis ball flight on a simple court. Add tennis_robot_sim.sim.ball with projectile motion under gravity, optional bounce, and landing-point ground intersection. Add tennis_robot_sim.sim.scenario with seeded scenarios: default, high_lob, fast_cross, noisy_camera. The simulator should output time, 3D ball position, and ground-truth landing point.
acceptance_criteria: A default scenario produces at least 60 time steps, a plausible landing point on the court plane, and deterministic output with the same seed. Unit tests verify gravity, landing calculation, and bounce behavior within tolerance.
safety_constraints: Simulation-first only; do not trigger real motor/GPIO/serial writes unless an explicit --hardware or --real flag is passed; preserve local git changes; do not run destructive git reset/clean/rm commands.

goal_title: Implement camera projection and synthetic frame renderer
codex_goal_prompt: Add a pinhole camera model and renderer. Implement world_to_pixel, pixel_to_ground_ray or homography helper, and render synthetic frames with a green/yellow tennis ball on a court-like background. The renderer should optionally add blur, occlusion, and noise. Save frames or video under outputs/synthetic/ for debugging.
acceptance_criteria: A script or module can render a short synthetic sequence; projected ball centers stay inside frame for default scenario; tests check projection shapes and inverse ground mapping.
validation_command: python -m pytest tests/test_camera_sim.py -q; python -m tennis_robot_sim.tools.render_synthetic --frames 30 --output outputs/synthetic/default.mp4 || true
safety_constraints: Simulation-first only; do not trigger real motor/GPIO/serial writes unless an explicit --hardware or --real flag is passed; preserve local git changes; do not run destructive git reset/clean/rm commands.

codex_goal_prompt: Implement a robust baseline detector for synthetic and simple real videos. Add tennis_robot_sim.perception.detector with a Detector interface and ColorBallDetector using HSV color threshold, morphology, contour filtering, circularity, radius limits, and confidence. Return detections with frame_id, timestamp, center_px, radius_px, confidence. Include a model-adapter stub for future neural detector but keep baseline CPU-only.
acceptance_criteria: Detector finds the synthetic tennis ball in at least 90 percent of default frames; false detections are filtered by area/radius; output schema is documented and tested.
safety_constraints: Do not download large model weights unless explicitly configured. Baseline must run CPU-only.

codex_goal_prompt: Add tennis_robot_sim.perception.tracker with a simple constant-velocity Kalman filter or exponential smoother for ball pixel tracks. It should accept detections, handle missing frames, reject outliers, and output smoothed center_px plus velocity_px_s and confidence. Keep implementation dependency-light using numpy/scipy only.
acceptance_criteria: Tracker reduces jitter on noisy synthetic detections; handles at least 5 consecutive missing detections without crashing; tests cover update, predict, and outlier rejection.
safety_constraints: Simulation-first only; do not trigger real motor/GPIO/serial writes unless an explicit --hardware or --real flag is passed; preserve local git changes; do not run destructive git reset/clean/rm commands.

goal_title: Map image detections to world rays or ground coordinates
codex_goal_prompt: Implement tennis_robot_sim.estimation.geometry to convert detections into camera rays and approximate ground-plane coordinates using camera config. Provide helpers for homography-based pixel_to_ground if available, and ray-plane intersection otherwise. Include calibration placeholders and clear assumptions.
acceptance_criteria: Given known synthetic camera parameters, converting projected ground points back to world ground coordinates is accurate within a small tolerance. Tests pass for center and corner points.
safety_constraints: Simulation-first only; do not trigger real motor/GPIO/serial writes unless an explicit --hardware or --real flag is passed; preserve local git changes; do not run destructive git reset/clean/rm commands.

codex_goal_prompt: Implement tennis_robot_sim.estimation.trajectory. Use timestamped detections plus camera geometry to estimate ball trajectory and predict court-plane landing point. For synthetic mode, use known or approximated depth from rendered ground truth when available; otherwise implement a documented 2D/ground-plane fallback. Fit a parabolic model under gravity, estimate velocity, and output LandingPrediction with landing_xy, time_to_land, uncertainty, and debug metrics.
acceptance_criteria: On default synthetic scenario, predicted landing_xy is within configurable tolerance of ground truth, e.g. <0.5 m for clean synthetic data; tests include noisy observations and missing frames.
safety_constraints: Simulation-first only; do not trigger real motor/GPIO/serial writes unless an explicit --hardware or --real flag is passed; preserve local git changes; do not run destructive git reset/clean/rm commands.

goal_title: Implement robot kinematics and simulated chassis
codex_goal_prompt: Implement tennis_robot_sim.robot.kinematics and tennis_robot_sim.robot.sim_robot for a small ground vehicle. Support differential-drive or Ackermann-style model depending on existing code; default to differential drive if unspecified. State includes x, y, yaw, linear velocity, angular velocity. Enforce max speed, max angular rate, acceleration limits, and court bounds from config.
acceptance_criteria: A simulated robot can move from start pose toward a target without leaving bounds; tests verify forward motion, rotation, saturation, and deterministic integration.
safety_constraints: Simulation only. Any real motor interface must be a separate class and disabled by default.

codex_goal_prompt: Implement tennis_robot_sim.robot.planner. Convert predicted landing point into an interception target pose for the car, with optional offset so the catcher/racket reaches the point. Plan a feasible target under speed limits and time_to_land. If target cannot be reached before landing, choose best-effort closest point and mark reachable=false.
acceptance_criteria: Planner returns target pose, reachable flag, ETA, distance, and reason. Tests cover reachable target, unreachable target, and target clipping within court bounds.
safety_constraints: Simulation-first only; do not trigger real motor/GPIO/serial writes unless an explicit --hardware or --real flag is passed; preserve local git changes; do not run destructive git reset/clean/rm commands.

codex_goal_prompt: Implement tennis_robot_sim.robot.controller with a PID or pure-pursuit style controller that drives the simulated robot toward target pose. Output velocity commands v and omega, respecting safety limits. Include convergence conditions and timeout behavior. Expose Controller.step(state, target, dt).
acceptance_criteria: Controller drives robot within tolerance of reachable target in simulation; commands are bounded by safety limits; tests verify convergence and saturation.
safety_constraints: Never send commands to real hardware from this controller unless routed through explicit hardware interface with enable flag.

codex_goal_prompt: Implement tennis_robot_sim.imu.sim_imu. Generate accelerometer and gyroscope readings from simulated robot motion with configurable bias, Gaussian noise, drift, and timestamp jitter. Include calibration parameters in config. Output IMUSample timestamp, accel, gyro, yaw_rate, optional ground truth pose for evaluation.
acceptance_criteria: IMU simulator produces realistic noisy readings from known robot trajectory; tests check zero-motion noise, constant rotation gyro sign, and deterministic seed behavior.
safety_constraints: Simulation-first only; do not trigger real motor/GPIO/serial writes unless an explicit --hardware or --real flag is passed; preserve local git changes; do not run destructive git reset/clean/rm commands.

codex_goal_prompt: Implement tennis_robot_sim.imu.localization. Fuse wheel/command odometry and IMU yaw-rate/acceleration to estimate car pose. Start with a complementary filter or lightweight EKF. Provide reset, predict, update_imu, update_odometry, and get_state methods. Report covariance or confidence if implemented.
acceptance_criteria: Localization tracks simulated robot pose with lower yaw error than odometry-only under noisy commands; tests compare estimated pose to ground truth over a short trajectory.
safety_constraints: Do not assume IMU gives absolute position. Clearly document drift and limitations.

codex_goal_prompt: Create the main closed-loop runner tennis_robot_sim.run_sim. It should load config, create scenario, render frames, detect and track tennis ball, estimate landing point, plan target, run robot controller with IMU-aided localization, log each step, and save outputs. CLI flags: --scenario, --config, --output, --seed, --frames, --no-hardware. It must default to no hardware.
acceptance_criteria: python -m tennis_robot_sim.run_sim --scenario default --no-hardware --output outputs/smoke completes successfully and writes metrics.json, trajectory.csv, robot_path.csv, and at least one visualization file.
safety_constraints: Default must be simulation/no-hardware. Hardware code must require explicit --hardware plus config safety.enable_real_hardware=true.

codex_goal_prompt: Add visualization tools for debugging. Save overlay frames or video showing detection circle, tracked path, predicted landing point, and robot target. acceptance_criteria: After run_sim, outputs include overlay image/video and top_down.png. Visuals are generated without GUI display and work on headless server.
validation_command: python -m tennis_robot_sim.run_sim --scenario default --no-hardware --output outputs/vis_test && ls outputs/vis_test

acceptance_criteria: End-to-end test passes on CPU in under about 60 seconds; failing conditions print useful debug paths.
safety_constraints: Simulation-first only; do not trigger real motor/GPIO/serial writes unless an explicit --hardware or --real flag is passed; preserve local git changes; do not run destructive git reset/clean/rm commands.

codex_goal_prompt: Add a single validation script scripts/validate_sim.sh that runs environment check, unit tests, and a short smoke simulation. It should be safe to run repeatedly and should not require GPU, camera, serial port, GPIO, or internet after dependencies are installed.

safety_constraints: No hardware access, no sudo, no destructive cleanup. Use mkdir -p outputs/validation instead of deleting broad directories.

codex_goal_prompt: If hardware control files exist, wrap them behind a RobotInterface with SimRobotInterface and RealRobotInterface. The default runner must use SimRobotInterface.
